problem: Previous npm account access is blocked; package publishing and enterprise runner builds must not depend on that account recovery.

internalRegistry.credentialModel: AWS IAM session auth; no long-lived NPM_TOKEN required

publicNpm.systemOfRecord: npm account/org + bridge-os Baseline vault for tokens
publicNpm.preferredAction: Create a new npm account/org and secure it with 2FA while old account recovery runs in parallel.
publicNpm.fallbackIfScopeUnavailable: Use new public scope or publish public packages after old scope recovery.

decision: Run both lanes: CodeArtifact unblocks internal builds; new npm account handles public distribution and recovery fallback.
nonGoals: Do not wait for old npm account recovery to unblock internal CI.
Do not treat AWS Secrets Manager as the npm credential source of truth.
Do not commit npm or CodeArtifact auth tokens.
